sections.title: Portfolio Overview
sections.prose: UAE BNPL portfolio overview. Smaller and newer than KSA — $77M outstanding across 981K loans. Single securitization facility ($131M, Goldman Sachs only). All monetary values in AED unless otherwise stated.

metrics.name: Total Outstanding AR
metrics.formula: Gross trade receivables from UAE entity
metrics.rationale: Size of UAE collateral pool — roughly 5% of KSA portfolio.

metrics.name: Repeat Customer Rate
metrics.formula: Repeat obligor balance / Total obligor balance
metrics.rationale: UAE at 80% repeat — slightly lower than KSA (83%).

metrics.name: Average Balance
metrics.formula: Total outstanding / Number of active loans
metrics.rationale: UAE avg $79 vs KSA $133 — smaller transaction sizes.

notes: UAE facility consistently shows Senior Advance Rate breach requiring monthly repayment — structural feature, not alarm signal.

sections.title: Covenant Compliance
sections.prose: UAE facility has wider covenant thresholds than KSA reflecting the newer, smaller portfolio. Delinquency L1: 10% (vs KSA 9%), Default L1: 8% (vs KSA 5%), Dilution L1: 15% (vs KSA 7.5%). Corporate covenants: Min Liquidity $30M, Min TNW $30M, Max D/E 2.0x.

metrics.name: Dilution Rate
metrics.rationale: UAE dilution runs 7.7-8.6% — structurally higher than KSA (~1.5%), driven by cross-border e-commerce returns. Well within 15% L1 threshold.

notes: UAE default rates were elevated for May-Jul 2025 vintages (2.6-3.7%) but improving rapidly. UAE facility tenor is only 2 years (vs KSA 3 years).

sections.title: Data Notes & Sources
sections.prose: UAE data sourced from same data room as KSA. Fewer data dimensions available — no demographic breakdowns, no BNPL+ deep dive (product not yet launched in UAE as of data room date), no separate business plan.
notes: UAE BNPL+ launch planned for Oct 2025 per Product & Regulation deck
UAE facility: 2 tranches only (Senior $100M + Junior $1M), Goldman Sachs sole lender
UAE dilution structurally higher (~8% vs ~1.5%) — likely cross-border e-commerce returns
UAE Ever-90 rate: 5.47% (vs KSA 2.46%) — higher credit risk in UAE portfolio
